text:
*[Bug 19049] btpe implementation rbinom has incorrect signs for error terms in Stirling approximation*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=19049>

--- Comment #3 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
(In reply to Kate Hudson from comment #2)
Thank you, Kate.
I have worked at finding a case, too;  it was relatively easy, just translating
the C code to R and run both versions (old and new).
Code reading (and a bit of thinking) also shows e.g., that 
 npq = n*p*(1-p) must be >= 46  for the "squeezing" case.

I've not yet replied here, as with my R code, I have started to "analyze" the
effect of the bug fix, but it will definitely will happen. 

I did confirm your PR already anyway, so there's not more to be done on your
side, thank you again!

elements.text: [Bug 19049] btpe implementation rbinom has incorrect signs for error terms in Stirling approximation

) ---
(In reply to Kate Hudson from comment #2)
Thank you, Kate.
I have worked at finding a case, too;  it was relatively easy, just translating
the C code to R and run both versions (old and new).
Code reading (and a bit of thinking) also shows e.g., that 
 npq = n*p*(1-p) must be >= 46  for the "squeezing" case.

I've not yet replied here, as with my R code, I have started to "analyze" the
effect of the bug fix, but it will definitely will happen. 

I did confirm your PR already anyway, so there's not more to be done on your
side, thank you again!

Indeed, thank you Suharto, for the suggestion... and I did port it in time.

*[Bug 18693] rmultinom()  using LDOUBLE is platform dependent*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18693>

Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) changed:

           What    |Removed                     |Added
----------------------------------------------------------------------------
         Resolution|---                         |FIXED
             Status|CONFIRMED                   |CLOSED

--- Comment #17 from Martin Maechler (<mailto:maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch|maechler@stat.math.ethz.ch>) ---
Fix committed, to R-devel svn rev 89909.
Thank you, Mauricio, and Ivan (teaching Mauricio and possibly others how to
create a correct patch).

As this changes `rmultinom()` results quite a bit on M<n> equipped Macs, or
other 
"no proper long double" platforms, I think we shold keep this in "R-devel"
which would mean that it would be only in released R in about a year ..

text:
*[Bug 18989] Wishlist: provide conversion of POSIXlt time zone retaining sub-second accuracy*
<https://bugs.r-project.org/show_bug.cgi?id=18989>

--- Comment #16 from Suharto Anggono (<mailto:suharto_anggono@yahoo.com|suharto_anggono@yahoo.com>) ---
(In reply to Martin Maechler from comment #13)
> Created attachment 3665
> "Test code R script"  (MM)

c(xx) == xx
R devel: TRUE for the last, FALSE elsewhere
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
floor sec before as.POSIXct: all TRUE

c(xx) - xx
R devel: 0 for the last, 1 sec elsewhere
R 4.5.3: all 0
floor sec before as.POSIXct: all 0

c(xx)$sec == xx$sec
R devel: all FALSE
R 4.5.3: all FALSE
floor sec before as.POSIXct: FALSE for the last, TRUE elsewhere

c(x) == x
R devel:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE at 8 to 23
i=6: FALSE at 7 to 23
i=7: FALSE at 6 to 23
i=8: FALSE at 5 to 23
i=9: FALSE at 3 to 23
i=10: FALSE at 1 to 23
i=11: all TRUE
R 4.5.3: all TRUE
floor sec before as.POSIXct: all TRUE

c(x)$sec[1:23] == x$sec[1:23]
R devel:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
R 4.5.3:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE
floor sec before as.POSIXct:
i=1 to i=10: all TRUE
i=11: all FALSE

R devel: all FALSE
R 4.5.3: all FALSE
floor sec before as.POSIXct: FALSE for the last, TRUE elsewhere

c(x) == x
R devel:
i=1 to i=5: FALSE at 8 to 23
i=6: FALSE at 7 to 23
i=7: FALSE at 6 to 23
i=8: FALSE at 5 to 23
i=9: FALSE at 3 to 23
i=10: FALSE at 1 to 23
i=11: all TRUE
R 4.5.3: all TRUE

i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE

i=1 to i=5: FALSE from 8
i=6: FALSE from 7
i=7: FALSE from 6
i=8: FALSE from 5
i=9: FALSE from 3
i=10 to i=11: all FALSE

i=1 to i=10: all TRUE
i=11: all FALSE
